title: BigBear.ai Announces First Quarter 2026 Results, Increases Backlog Including More Than $60 million in National Security Contracts
text: BigBear.ai reported first-quarter 2026 financial results highlighting significant progress in national security contracts and operational improvements. The defense technology company generated revenue of $34.4 million in Q1 2026, relatively flat compared to $34.8 million in the prior year. However, gross margins showed substantial improvement, expanding to 34.0% from 21.3% year-over-year—a gain of approximately 1,278 basis points. CEO Kevin McAleenan stated: These wins keep us on track to meet our topline revenue target for 2026. The company's backlog increased 14% to $281.9 million, primarily driven by a significant sole-source classified award valued at $53 million. Management noted approximately $75 million in total wins during the quarter, with more than $60 million categorized as national security contracts. BigBear.ai eliminated $124.6 million in outstanding 2029 Convertible notes through debt-to-equity conversions in January 2026, reducing annual interest expenses by $4.8 million. The company maintains a strong liquidity position with $431.5 million in available cash and investments as of March 31, 2026. Management affirmed full-year 2026 revenue guidance of $135 million to $165 million, reflecting confidence in execution despite ongoing organizational realignment efforts.
